title: GFCI Protection - Dwelling Units
text: All 125-volt through 250-volt receptacles supplied by single-phase branch circuits rated 150 volts or less to ground installed in the following locations of dwelling units shall have ground-fault circuit-interrupter protection for personnel: bathrooms; garages and accessory buildings; outdoors; crawl spaces at or below grade; basements; kitchens; sinks (within 6 ft); boathouses; bathtubs or shower stalls; laundry areas; indoor damp and wet locations; receptacles within 6 ft of the top inside edge of bowl of plumbing fixtures.

title: GFCI Protection - Other Than Dwelling Units
text: All 125-volt through 250-volt receptacles supplied by single-phase branch circuits rated 150 volts or less to ground, 50 amperes or less, installed in the following locations other than dwelling units shall have GFCI protection: bathrooms; kitchens; rooftops; outdoors; sinks (within 6 ft); indoor wet locations; locker rooms with shower facilities; garages, service bays, and similar areas; crawl spaces at or below grade; unfinished portions of basements; laundry areas.

title: AFCI Protection
text: All 120-volt, single-phase, 15- and 20-ampere branch circuits supplying outlets or devices installed in dwelling unit kitchens, family rooms, dining rooms, living rooms, parlors, libraries, dens, bedrooms, sunrooms, recreation rooms, closets, hallways, laundry areas, or similar rooms or areas shall be protected by any of the means described in 210.12(A)(1) through (6).

title: Dwelling Unit Receptacle Spacing - General
text: Receptacles in every kitchen, family room, dining room, living room, parlor, library, den, sunroom, bedroom, recreation room, or similar room or area of dwelling units shall be installed such that no point measured horizontally along the floor line of any wall space is more than 6 feet (1.8 m), measured horizontally, from a receptacle outlet. A wall space is defined as any space 2 feet (600 mm) or more in width and unbroken by doorways, fireplaces, etc.

title: Kitchen Countertop Receptacles
text: In kitchens, pantries, breakfast rooms, dining rooms, and similar areas of dwelling units, receptacle outlets for countertop and work surfaces shall be installed at each wall countertop or work surface 12 inches (300 mm) or wider. Outlets shall be installed so no point along the wall line is more than 24 inches (600 mm), measured horizontally, from a receptacle. At least two 20-amp small-appliance branch circuits per 210.11(C)(1) required.

title: Service Disconnect Location
text: Means shall be provided to disconnect all conductors in a building or other structure from the service-entrance conductors. The service disconnecting means shall be installed at a readily accessible location either outside of a building or structure or inside nearest the point of entrance of the service conductors.

title: Grounding Electrode System
text: All grounding electrodes described in 250.52(A)(1) through (A)(7) that are present at each building or structure served shall be bonded together to form the grounding electrode system. Where none of these grounding electrodes exist, one or more of the grounding electrodes specified in 250.52(A)(4) through (A)(8) shall be installed and used.

title: Conductor Ampacity
text: Allowable ampacities of insulated conductors rated up to 2000 volts, not more than three current-carrying conductors in raceway, cable, or earth (directly buried), based on ambient temperature of 30 deg C (86 deg F). At 75C insulation: 14 AWG Cu = 20 A (but limited to 15 A per 240.4(D)), 12 AWG Cu = 25 A (limited to 20 A), 10 AWG Cu = 35 A (limited to 30 A), 8 AWG = 50 A, 6 AWG = 65 A, 4 AWG = 85 A.

title: Tamper-Resistant Receptacles
text: All nonlocking-type 125-volt and 250-volt, 15- and 20-ampere receptacles in dwelling units, hotels and motels guest rooms, child care facilities, preschools, elementary education facilities, business offices, corridors, waiting rooms (medical and dental), assembly occupancies, and dormitory units shall be listed tamper-resistant receptacles.

title: Working Space at Electrical Equipment
text: Working space for equipment operating at 1000V nominal or less to ground and likely to require examination, adjustment, servicing, or maintenance while energized shall be provided. Depth of working space: 3 ft minimum (Condition 1, no exposed live parts opposite). Width: 30 inches or width of equipment, whichever greater. Height: 6.5 ft minimum or height of equipment.
